---
title: CV - [First Name Last Name]
author: "First Last"
format:
  quarto-cv-typst: default
date: last-modified # use last-modified for auto update, or set a specific date (e.g. 2026-01-01)
date-prefix: "Last updated: " # leave empty ("") to hide the prefix
execute:
  echo: false

# =============================================================================
# STEP 1 — Choose a theme (uncomment one line)
# =============================================================================
# Each theme sets colors, fonts, spacing and layout.
# Only one line should be active at a time.
metadata-files:
  # - themes/classic-blue.yml    # blue professional (default)
  # - themes/forest-green.yml    # natural green
  - themes/minimal-dark.yml    # dark sidebar
  # - themes/warm-terracotta.yml # earthy terracotta

# =============================================================================
# STEP 2 — Your personal info
# =============================================================================
# Fields not listed here are simply hidden. Remove any line you don't need.
sidebar:
  photo: "assets/img/image.png"
  phone: "XX XX XX XX XX"
  email: "fname.lname@email.com"
  birthdate: "DD/MM/YYYY"
  city: "City, Country"
  nationality: "Nationality"
  website: "https://www.mywebsite.com"
  # website-display: "My Website" # Optional: custom display text for the link
  permit: "Driving License B"
  linkedin: "https://linkedin.com/in/username"
  linkedin-display: "\\@username" # Optional: custom display text
  github: "https://github.com/username"
  github-display: "\\@username"   # Optional: custom display text

# =============================================================================
# STEP 3 — Sidebar sections (reorder or comment out to hide)
# =============================================================================
# - Change the order of sections by moving blocks up or down.
# - Comment out an entire block (- id: ...) to hide that section.
# - Each section accepts a style: block to override spacing, font, colors.
#   See the ADVANCED section below or the README for all style options.
#
# You can also add your own custom sections with any id and title:
#
#   - id: my-section
#     title: "My Custom Section"
#     items:
#       - "Free text item"
#       - name: "Key"
#         value: "Value"
#       - name: "Category"
#         subitems:
#           - "Sub-item 1"
#           - "Sub-item 2"
#
sidebar-sections:

  # --- Profile photo ---
  - id: photo
    style:
      section-before: 2em
      photo-size: 100pt
      photo-radius: 50%         # 50% = circle, 0% = square
      photo-border: true        # true/false
      photo-border-width: 4pt
      section-after: 3em

  # --- Contact info (uses sidebar: fields above) ---
  # Available types: phone, email, birthdate, city, nationality, permit,
  #                  website, linkedin, github
  - id: contact
    title: "Contact"
    items:
      - type: phone
      - type: email
      - type: birthdate
      - type: city
      - type: nationality
      - type: permit
      - type: linkedin
    style:
      item-after: 0em
      section-after: 3em
      # icon-color: "#ff9500"  # Override icon color for this section only
      # icon-size: 10pt
      # title-size: 18pt

  # --- Free text + link (e.g. website callout) ---
  - id: details
    title: "More Info"
    items:
      - "[Visit my website for more details about my background and work.]"
      - type: website
    style:
      text-size: 9.5pt

  # --- Language / level pairs ---
  - id: languages
    title: "Languages"
    items:
      - name: "[Language 1]"
        value: "[Level]"
      - name: "[Language 2]"
        value: "[Level]"
    style:
      text-size: 10.5pt

  # --- Plain bullet list ---
  - id: strengths
    title: "Strengths"
    items:
      - "[Strength 1]"
      - "[Strength 2]"
      - "[Strength 3]"
      - "[Strength 4]"
    style:
      text-size: 10.5pt

  # --- Nested interests (name + subitems) ---
  - id: interests
    title: "Interests"
    items:
      - name: "[Interest 1]"
        subitems:
          - "[Sub-interest 1]"
          - "[Sub-interest 2]"
          - "[Sub-interest 3]"
      - name: "[Interest 2]"
        subitems:
          - "[Sub-interest 1]"
          - "[Sub-interest 2]"
      - name: "[Interest 3]"
    style:
      text-size: 10.5pt

  # --- Skill categories (category: label + inline list) ---
  - id: skills
    title: "IT Skills"
    items:
      - category: "Languages"
        items: "[Language 1, Language 2]"
      - category: "Tools"
        items: "[Tool 1, Tool 2]"
    style:
      text-size: 10.5pt
      section-before: 5em

  # --- Example: commented-out optional sections ---

  # - id: certifications
  #   title: "Certifications"
  #   items:
  #     - name: "AWS Solutions Architect"
  #       value: "2023"
  #     - name: "Google Cloud Professional"
  #       value: "2024"
  #   style:
  #     text-size: 8pt

  # - id: networks
  #   title: "Networks"
  #   items:
  #     - type: linkedin
  #     - type: github

# =============================================================================
# STEP 4 — Header: name, job title and objective (shown at the top of the CV)
# =============================================================================
# Colors inherit from the active theme. Override any field optionally.
cv-header:
  name: "[First Name Last Name]"
  title: "[Job Title or Research Position]"
  objective: >-
    [Your professional goal. Describe your background, motivations and
    what you are looking for in 2-3 sentences.]
  # Optional color/size overrides (theme defaults apply if omitted):
  # name-color: "#000000"
  # name-size: "14pt"
  # title-color: "#2563eb"        # defaults to cv-theme.main.title-color
  # title-size: "18pt"
  # objective-bg: "#f1f5f9"       # defaults to cv-layout.sidebar-bg
  # objective-stroke-color: "#2563eb"   # defaults to cv-theme.main.title-color
  # objective-stroke-width: "2pt"

# =============================================================================
# ADVANCED — Override theme values (uncomment to customize)
# =============================================================================
# These blocks let you fine-tune the active theme without editing the theme
# file. Only the keys you define here are overridden — the rest come from
# the theme preset chosen in STEP 1.

# --- Colors & fonts ---
# cv-theme:
#   main-font: "Poppins"        # main content font (Inter also bundled)
#   title-font: "Poppins"       # sidebar section title font
#   text-font: "Poppins"        # sidebar body text font
#
#   sidebar:                    # colors for the LEFT column
#     title-color: "#2563eb"    # → section titles (Contact, Languages…) + photo border
#     text-color: "#1e293b"     # → plain items, names, key labels
#     accent-color: "#64748b"   # → values (language levels), sub-items ◦
#     link-color: "#2563eb"     # → phone, email, @username, website links
#
#   main:                       # colors for the RIGHT column
#     title-color: "#2563eb"    # → H1 headings (Education, Experience…)
#     subtitle-color: "#1e293b" # → H2 headings (Position, Degree…)
#     text-color: "#1e293b"     # → body text
#     # link-color: none        # leave unset → use {color="..."} on inline links
#
#   headings:                   # font sizes in the right column
#     h1: 14pt                  # section titles
#     h2: 11pt                  # entry titles
#     h3: 10pt                  # details (company, dates…)
#     normal: 10pt
#     # h4: 9pt
#     # h5-h6: 8pt

# --- Sidebar spacing (global defaults for all sections) ---
# sidebar-defaults:
#   text-size: 9pt
#   title-size: 11pt
#   icon-size: 11pt
#   title-after: 1em           # space below section title
#   item-after: 1em            # space between items
#   section-after: 3em         # space below entire section

# --- Layout & page ---
# cv-layout:
#   sidebar-width: 30%         # e.g. 30%, 5cm, 1fr
#   main-width: 2.5fr
#   gutter: 1cm                # space between columns
#   sidebar-bg: "#f1f5f9"
#   main-bg: "#ffffff"
#   margins:
#     top: 0.5cm
#     bottom: 0.5cm
#     left: 0.5cm
#     right: 0.5cm
#   timeline:
#     dot-size: "3pt"
#     line-width: "1pt"
#     # dot-color: "#2563eb"
#     # line-color: "#94a3b8"
---

# Education

::: {.timeline }

## YYYY - YYYY | [Degree Title]

*[Institution, City] | [Grade/Honours]*

[Description of the program content.]

## YYYY - YYYY | [Degree Title]

*[Institution, City] | [Grade/Honours]*

[Description of the program content.]

## YYYY - YYYY | [Degree Title]

*[Institution, City] | [Grade/Honours]*

:::

# Professional Experience

::: {.timeline levels="2,3,4"}

## Mon. YYYY - Mon. YYYY | [Job Title]

*[Lab / Organization, City]*

[Description of the mission and tasks performed ([Project Name]).]


:::



# Skills

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 2
#| fig-height: 2
#| out-width: "30%"
#| fig-align: "center"


library(ggplot2)

skills <- data.frame(
  skill = factor(
    c("R", "Python", "SQL", "Git", "Typst"),
    levels = c("Typst", "Git", "SQL", "Python", "R")
  ),
  level = c(90, 75, 80, 70, 60)
)

ggplot(skills, aes(x = skill, y = level)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#BA1634", width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(level, "%")), hjust = -0.2, size = 3, color = "grey30") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 105), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x        = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks         = element_blank(),
    plot.margin        = margin(4, 20, 4, 4)
  )
```



# Publications
<!-- 
:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="45%"}

Left content — takes 45% of the width.

```{python}
#| fig-width: 4
#| fig-height: 2
#| out-width: "100%"
#| fig-cap: "Publications par année"

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

years = [2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025]
count = [1, 2, 1, 3, 2]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(4, 2))
ax.bar(years, count, color="#BA1634", width=0.5, zorder=2)
ax.set_xticks(years)
ax.set_yticks(range(0, max(count) + 2))
ax.set_xlabel(None)
ax.set_ylabel("Publications", fontsize=8)
ax.grid(axis="y", linewidth=0.4, color="#e5e5e5", zorder=1)
ax.spines[["top", "right", "left"]].set_visible(False)
ax.tick_params(labelsize=8)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```
::: -->

<!-- ::: {.column width="55%"}
Right content — takes 55% of the width.

| Year | Title | Venue |
|------|-------|-------|
| 2025 | [Title A](https://arxiv.org){color="#b31b1b"} | Journal X |
| 2024 | [Title B](https://arxiv.org){color="#b31b1b"} | Conf. Y |
| 2024 | [Title C](https://arxiv.org){color="#b31b1b"} | Conf. Z |
| 2023 | [Title D](https://arxiv.org){color="#b31b1b"} | Workshop |
: Sélection de publications
:::

::::

::: {.callout-note}

Ceci est un exemple de callout. Les types disponibles sont : callout-note, callout-tip, callout-warning, callout-important, callout-caution.
::: -->
<!-- 
::: {.list-table}

- - header 1
  - header 2

- - row 1 col 1
  - row 1 col 2

- - row 2 col 1
  - row 2 col 2
: test {#tbl-second}
:::

::: {.callout-note}

test 

::: -->


<!-- ::: callout-note
test
::: -->

::: list-table
- 

  - header 1
  - header 2

- 

  - row 1 col 1
  - row 1 col 2

- 

  - row 2 col 1
  - row 2 col 2
:::

::: {#tbl-panel layout-ncol="2"}
| Col1 | Col2 | Col3 |
|------|------|------|
| A    | B    | C    |
| E    | F    | G    |
| A    | G    | G    |

: First Table

| Col1 | Col2 | Col3 |
|------|------|------|
| A    | B    | C    |
| E    | F    | G    |
| A    | G    | G    |

: Second Table

Main Caption
:::

See @tbl-panel for details

<!-- ::: {.column-margin}

Cette légende est dans la marge.
::: -->


::: {layout="[60, 40]"}
```{=typst}
See my #link("[https://example.com](https://example.com)")[#text(fill: rgb("#2563eb"))[portfolio]] for details.
```

See my [[portfolio]{style="color: #2563eb;"}](https://example.com) for details. 
:::
